title: Flowise replaceInputsWithConfig FILE-STORAGE:: bypass allows overrideConfig MCP env injection leading to RCE
description: In replaceInputsWithConfig() in packages/server/src/utils/index.ts, when applying a chatflow's overrideConfig to node inputs, the code skips the isParameterEnabled() authorization check for any config value whose string contains the literal 'FILE-STORAGE::'. The check uses String.includes() rather than startsWith() and performs no parameter-type validation, so an attacker can prepend a comment such as '/* FILE-STORAGE:: */' to an arbitrary parameter (e.g. mcpServerConfig) and have it accepted even though the administrator never enabled it for override. On a public chatflow that contains a Custom MCP tool node with API Override enabled, an unauthenticated /api/v1/prediction request can therefore override mcpServerConfig and inject an env block containing NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-loader (which the MCP node's dangerousEnvVars blocklist does not cover), causing the spawned MCP child process to execute attacker-supplied JavaScript and arbitrary OS commands.
